Particulars Of the political confer
ence in Spokane on Saturday, July %,
Immediately following the democratic
county convention, have been publish
ed in the newspapers of the state
from time to time.


The participants in the notable
conference were Nelson w. Durham,
editor Of the Spokesman-Review
George Stevenson, railroad lobbyist
for the Oregon Railroad e. Naviga-
ton company. and George Turner,
democratic candidate for governor.


The facts have not been denied by
the Spokesman-Review nor any par
ticipant in the meeting.


Mr.. Durham does not deny that as
result of the conference Mr.. Stew
enson agreed to exert the influences
he controlled to secure the nomina-
ton and election Of George Turner
for governor.


Mr.. Durham does nOA deny that he
promised at that conference to make
no reference in the Spokesman-Re-
